title: Don't show PASS on success for gitvalidate
body:
Make test logs less verbose so that failures are easier to spot.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: Ok, I thought the pipefail would exit with != 0.

It does, but remember that `make` executes every separate line in a separate shell; so a lone `set -o pipefail` on its own line will not affect the next one. Very quick testing on my end shows this to be more promising:
```make
    set -o pipefail; GIT_CHECK_EXCLUDE=...
```

Long rambling notes ahead, caveat lector.

First, it turns out that `vbatts/git-validation` has both `-v` and `-q` flags, and we're invoking it with `-v`. That goes a long way toward explaining the verbosity. After playing with removing `-v` and/or adding `-q`, I think the most helpful output comes from simply removing `-v`, **not** adding `-q`, and changing the `grep` in your PR to:
```
          ... | { egrep -v ' PASS$$' || test $$? = 1; }
```
(that is, use `egrep` instead of grep, and then single-quote, space, PASS, dollar, dollar, single quote). The reason for that is that just grepping out `PASS` would filter any commit message containing the string PASS - such as this one.

Going further, I think the ultimate root cause here is the hardcoding of `EPOCH_TEST_COMMIT`. Here's another of my stupid questions: why not just use `master`?
```make
.gitvalidation: .gopathok
	GIT_CHECK_EXCLUDE="./vendor:docs/make.bat" $(GOBIN)/git-validation -run DCO,short-subject,dangling-whitespace -range master..$(HEAD)
```
This produces clean concise readable results, and doesn't make me as nervous as that `pipefail - grep` chain.
...or, on reflection, `master^` to better handle merges?

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

How about this (on `Makefile:6`) and removing the `-v` on the `git-validation` call (line 470)?
```make
EPOCH_TEST_COMMIT ?= $(shell git merge-base HEAD $${DEST_BRANCH:-master})
```
Oh - what I was suggesting is that with the `git merge-base` you no longer need the `pipefail` nor `grep -v`
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #4777) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
LGTM! Before merging: @cevich, could you take a look at the `EPOCH` change and give it your blessing?

>  could you take a look at the EPOCH change and give it your blessing?

I was too late/slow :cry: I think my concerns were minor though, given that we already run `git` all over the place in the Makefile and nothing broke before this merged :smile:
